title: The Ultimate Guide to 10 Herbal Tinctures for Bad Breath

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Rosmarinic acid is good for bad breath because it has antibacterial properties that help kill the bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath and gum disease.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, is a natural herb that helps to freshen bad breath. This is because peppermint oil has antibacterial properties, which kill the germs in the mouth that cause bad breath. When we eat or drink, tiny bits of food can get stuck in our teeth and gums, attracting bacteria that feed on them and release a bad smell. Peppermint oil helps to break down these food particles and kill the bacteria, leaving the mouth feeling clean and fresh. Additionally, peppermint oil has a cooling effect that can help to numb the gums and reduce inflammation, which can also contribute to bad breath. When used as a tincture, peppermint oil can be easily absorbed into the bloodstream, providing quick relief from bad breath.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Coriandrum sativum herbal tinctures that help with bad breath, you'll need some fresh or dried coriander seeds, a glass jar with a lid, and a solvent like vodka or glycerin. First, measure out a cup of coriander seeds and place them in the glass jar. Next, add a solvent that's at least two times the volume of the seeds, so for example, two cups of vodka. If you're using glycerin, be aware that it's a slower process. Close the jar tightly and let it sit in a cool, dark place for two to six weeks, shaking the jar every day or two. This will allow the coriander seeds to infuse their goodness into the solvent. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container, discarding the solids. Your coriander tincture is now ready to use - take a few drops under your tongue to help freshen your breath and reduce bad breath.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal tinctures for bad breath

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus tincture is good for bad breath because it has antimicrobial properties. This means it can kill the tiny germs that cause bad breath. The tincture contains chemicals called cineole and camphor, which are known to fight against these germs. When you put the tincture in your mouth, it can help to kill the bacteria that cause bad breath. This can be especially helpful for people who have gum disease or mouth infections, as these conditions can cause bad breath. Eucalyptus globulus tincture is also a natural and non-irritating way to freshen your breath, making it a good alternative to commercial mouthwashes that can contain harsh chemicals. The tincture can be mixed with water and swished around in the mouth before spitting it out, helping to kill germs and freshen breath naturally.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Coriandrum sativum herbal tincture to help with bad breath, you will need a few ingredients and some basic equipment. Start by gathering fresh Coriandrum sativum leaves and flowers, and gently wash them with clean water to remove any dirt or debris. Next, chop the Coriandrum sativum leaves and flowers into small pieces and fill a clean glass jar about one-quarter full. Then, pour 80-100 proof vodka or a high-proof spirit over the Coriandrum sativum leaves and flowers, making sure they are completely covered. Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two to help the herbs steep into the liquid. After the steeping period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. This will give you a strong Coriandrum sativum tincture that can be used to help eliminate bad breath. To use the tincture, mix a few drops with water and swish it around your mouth before spitting it out.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal tinctures for bad breath

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, commonly known as ginger, has been used for centuries to help with bad breath. The tincture of ginger contains active compounds that have antibacterial properties. These compounds help to fight the bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath. When the bacteria in the mouth are reduced, the production of volatile sulfur compounds, which are the main cause of bad breath, also decreases. Ginger tincture can help to reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, which can also contribute to bad breath. Additionally, ginger has natural antiseptic properties that can help to kill bacteria in the mouth and prevent the growth of new bacteria. This can help to keep the mouth and throat clean and fresh, reducing the occurrence of bad breath. Ginger tincture can be used as a natural remedy to help alleviate bad breath and promote good oral health.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Coriandrum sativum herbal tincture that helps with bad breath, start by gathering fresh or dried coriander seeds. You can purchase these at a local health food store or grow them in your garden. Next, combine one part coriander seeds with four parts of a solvent like vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar. Make sure the jar is filled to the top to prevent contamination. Seal the jar tightly and place it in a cool, dark place for at least two weeks, shaking the jar every day. This process will allow the active compounds in the coriander seeds to infuse into the solvent, creating the tincture. After two weeks, strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle. Discard the solids and store the tincture in a cool, dark place. To use the tincture, mix a few drops with water and swish it around in your mouth before spitting it out. This will help freshen your breath and reduce bad breath caused by bacteria in the mouth.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum zeylanicum, also known as Ceylon cinnamon, is a natural remedy for bad breath. The tincture is made from the bark of the cinnamon tree, which contains a compound called cinnamaldehyde. This compound has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to kill the bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath. When you use Ceylon cinnamon tincture, it helps to reduce the growth of these bacteria, preventing the formation of volatile sulfur compounds that cause bad breath. Additionally, the tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and irritation in the gums and mouth, which can also contribute to bad breath. By addressing the root causes of bad breath, Ceylon cinnamon tincture can provide long-lasting relief and help to keep your breath fresh and clean. The tincture can be used in various ways, such as adding a few drops to water or taking it as a dietary supplement.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Coriandrum sativum herbal tinctures that help with bad breath, you will need some fresh coriander leaves, a jar with a lid, vodka or another high-proof spirit, and a dark glass bottle with a dropper. First, harvest the fresh coriander leaves and chop them finely. Fill the jar with the chopped coriander leaves, leaving about an inch at the top. Pour vodka or the high-proof spirit over the coriander leaves, making sure that they are completely covered. Store the jar in a cool, dark place and let it sit for about 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two. This process is called 'steeping' and it helps to extract the active ingredients from the coriander leaves into the vodka. After the steeping process, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into the dark glass bottle with a dropper. Discard the solids and store the tincture in a cool, dark place. To use the tincture, place a few drops under your tongue or add it to a glass of water and drink it. The active ingredients in Coriandrum sativum are believed to have antibacterial properties, which can help to kill the bacteria that cause bad breath.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum tincture, made from the inner bark of the Ceylon cinnamon tree, is known for its antibacterial properties. It contains a compound called cinnamaldehyde, which helps kill the bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath. When we eat or drink something, bacteria in our mouth break down the food and release strong-smelling compounds. These compounds can make our breath smell bad. Cinnamomum verum tincture works by stopping the growth of these bacteria, which in turn reduces the production of bad-smelling compounds. This makes Cinnamomum verum tincture a good remedy for bad breath. The tincture also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and irritation in the gums and mouth, further contributing to fresher breath.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Coriandrum sativum herbal tincture that helps with bad breath, start by gathering fresh or dried Coriander seeds. You can use either one, but fresh seeds are more potent. Next, combine a handful of Coriander seeds with a 1:3 to 1:5 ratio of vodka or high-proof alcohol and a small amount of glycerin in a clean glass jar. For example, use 1 part Coriander seeds and 4 parts vodka. Close the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2 to 4 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two to help the Coriander seeds infuse into the liquid. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the Coriander seeds. Transfer the Coriander tincture to smaller dropper bottles and store them in a cool, dark place. To use the tincture, place a few drops under your tongue or add it to water to help alleviate bad breath.

Once dry, combine 100 grams of the dried plant material with 500 milliliters of vodka in a clean glass jar, making sure the plant is completely covered by the vodka. Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for two to three weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the vodka extract the plant's active compounds. The tincture of Coriandrum sativum contains antiseptic properties that help to kill these bacteria, reducing the amount of gas they release. intro_desc: Bad breath, also known as halitosis, can be a frustrating and embarrassing problem. It's not just about personal hygiene; bad breath can also be a sign of underlying health issues, such as gum disease, diabetes, or sinus infections. Fixing bad breath is important because it can improve your self-confidence and relationships with others. Herbal tinctures can be a natural and effective solution to this problem. Two herbs that are commonly used to combat bad breath are peppermint (Mentha x piperita) and eucalyptus (Eucalyptus globulus).  Peppermint oil has a strong antibacterial property that can help kill the bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath. It also has a refreshing and cooling effect, which can help to reduce inflammation and kill the bacteria that can cause bad breath. Eucalyptus oil, on the other hand, has a decongestant property that can help to clear out mucus and debris from the sinuses and mouth, which can help to reduce bad breath.  When you use herbal tinctures to fix bad breath, you can also improve your overall health and well-being. Bad breath can be a sign of an underlying health issue, and by addressing the root cause of the problem, you can prevent more serious health issues from developing. Additionally, using herbal tinctures can be a natural and chemical-free way to improve your oral health and prevent bad breath from coming back. By incorporating peppermint and eucalyptus into your oral care routine, you can say goodbye to bad breath and hello to a healthier, happier you.
supplementary_best: A blend of Eucalyptus globulus, Thymus vulgaris, and Cinnamomum verum tinctures can help remedy bad breath. Eucalyptus globulus has natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce plaque and soothe gums, reducing the causes of bad breath. Thymus vulgaris, or thyme, contains compounds that kill bacteria in the mouth, including those that produce volatile sulfur compounds, a common cause of bad breath. Cinnamomum verum, or Ceylon cinnamon, has antimicrobial properties that can combat bacteria and fungi in the mouth. The combination of these three herbs addresses multiple causes of bad breath, providing a comprehensive remedy.
supplementary_avoid: If you have bad breath, there are some herbal tinctures you should avoid using. One of these is Goldenseal (Hydrastis canadensis) because it can cause an increase in saliva production. Although this might sound like a good thing for bad breath, the excess saliva can actually make the breath smell worse if it contains bacteria or other odor-causing substances.  Another herbal tincture to avoid is Garlic (Allium sativum). While it is known for its antibacterial properties and is often used to freshen breath, using it as a tincture can be overwhelming and may not be pleasant for others. Additionally, excessive garlic consumption can lead to digestive issues and other health problems.  You should also be cautious when using Dandelion (Taraxacum officinale) root tincture. Although it is often used for digestive issues, it can cause stomach upset and gas, which may contribute to bad breath.  Lastly, Wormwood (Artemisia absinthium) tincture is not recommended for people with bad breath because it can cause dry mouth and stomach upset. This can lead to an increase in bacteria in the mouth, making bad breath worse.
supplementary_other_remedies: Bad breath, or halitosis, can be embarrassing and uncomfortable. Besides herbal tinctures, there are other natural remedies that can help. Oil pulling with coconut oil or sesame oil is one option. These oils have antibacterial properties that can kill the bacteria in your mouth, which is often the cause of bad breath. When you swish the oil around in your mouth, it can help remove food particles and bacteria, leaving your mouth feeling clean and fresh.  Another option is to use activated charcoal powder or tablets. These can help absorb the toxins and bacteria in your mouth, reducing bad breath. Some people also use baking soda as a natural toothpaste or mouthwash. Baking soda has antibacterial properties and can help neutralize the acid in your mouth, which can contribute to bad breath. Additionally, drinking plenty of water can help keep your mouth moist and wash away bacteria and food particles. This can be especially helpful if you have a dry mouth, which is a common cause of bad breath. Finally, some people find that chewing on fresh parsley or cilantro can help freshen their breath. These herbs have antibacterial properties and can help kill the bacteria in your mouth.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with bad breath often experience other health issues that can be treated with medicinal herbs. One common problem is gum disease, also known as gingivitis. This is an inflammation of the gums caused by poor oral hygiene and bacteria buildup. The medicinal herb, turmeric, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce gum inflammation and prevent further damage. Turmeric also contains a compound called curcumin, which has antibacterial properties that can kill the bacteria causing the bad breath.  Another issue people with bad breath may face is digestive problems, such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). This is often caused by an imbalance of gut bacteria, which can lead to symptoms like bloating, abdominal pain, and bad breath. The medicinal herb, peppermint, has a calming effect on the digestive system and can help reduce inflammation in the gut. Peppermint also has antibacterial properties that can help kill the bacteria causing bad breath.  Some people with bad breath may also experience sinus infections, which can cause bad breath, nasal congestion, and facial pain. The medicinal herb, eucalyptus, has decongestant properties that can help clear out mucus from the sinuses and reduce inflammation. Eucalyptus also has antibacterial properties that can help kill the bacteria causing bad breath.  People with bad breath may also experience dry mouth, which can be caused by dehydration, medication, or a lack of saliva production. The medicinal herb, licorice root, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe a dry, irritated mouth and throat. Licorice root also has antibacterial properties that can help kill the bacteria causing bad breath.  Finally, some people with bad breath may experience acid reflux, which can cause bad breath, heartburn, and chest pain. The medicinal herb, slippery elm, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the mouth and throat. Slippery elm also has demulcent properties that can help reduce inflammation and heal damaged tissues.  In addition, people with bad breath may also experience oral thrush, which is a fungal infection that can cause white patches on the tongue and inside the mouth. The medicinal herb, neem, has antifungal properties that can help kill the fungus causing oral thrush and reduce the risk of bad breath.  Note: Before using any medicinal herbs, it's recommended to consult with a healthcare professional to ensure safe and effective use.
